---
title: "HW 04b: Capstone Project Setup"
subtitle: "Set up your capstone project repository"
editor: source
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## {{< fa bullseye >}} Learning Objectives

By completing this assignment, you will:

1. Set up your personal capstone project repository on GitHub
2. Clone the repository to Posit Cloud
3. Create a basic directory structure for your project

## {{< fa terminal >}} Assignment Tasks

### Create a new repository on GitHub & clone to Posit Cloud

1. Open the GitHub Organisation for the course: [{{< var course.github-org >}}]({{< var course.github-org >}})

2. To the right of the field ["Find a repository"]{.highlight-yellow}, click on the green ["New"]{.highlight-yellow} button.

3. In the ["Repository name"]{.highlight-yellow} field write `capstone-project-username`. [Replace username with your GitHub username]{.highlight-yellow}. Avoid using spaces. For example: `capstone-project-rainbow-train` for the user with the username `rainbow-train`

4. Scroll down on the same page, and click ["Create repository"]{.highlight-yellow}.

5. In the ["Quick setup"]{.highlight-yellow} field, click on the clipboard next to the HTTPS URL 

6. Open the Posit Cloud workspace for the course: [{{< var course.code >}}]({{< var platforms.posit-cloud >}}content/all?sort=name_asc)

7. Open the **Content** page

8. Click on **New Project -> New Project from Git Repository**

9. Paste the HTTPS URL from GitHub into the ["URL of your Git Repository"]{.highlight-yellow} field.

10. Keep the **tick** next to **Add packages from the base project**

11. Click the **OK** button

12. Wait until the project is deployed.

### Set up initial directory structure

1. In your capstone project on Posit Cloud, create the following directory structure:
   - `data/` - for storing your datasets
   - `docs/` - for documentation and supplementary materials

2. You can create these directories by:
   - Using the Files panel in RStudio: Click "New Folder" and enter the folder names
   - Or using the terminal/console with: `dir.create("data")` and `dir.create("docs")`

### Create a README for your data folder

1. Navigate to the Files tab in the bottom right window of RStudio.

2. Open the `data` folder you just created.

3. Click on the "Blank File" button to create a new file.

4. Select "Text file".

5. Enter the name "README.md" and click OK.

6. Go to: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rbtl-dev/metadata-readme-template/main/README.md

7. Copy the content displayed in the browser and paste it into the `README.md` file.

8. Save the file.

### Commit and push your changes

1. In the Git panel (usually in the top right), stage all new files by checking the boxes next to them.

2. Click "Commit".

3. Write a meaningful commit message such as "Initial project setup with directory structure and data README".

4. Click "Commit".

5. Click "Push" to upload your changes to GitHub.

### Final step

Respond to the **Questions & Answer for the Capstone Project report** issue on the GitHub issue tracker of your repository and tag the course instructor using the `@` sign in combination with larnsce as the username.

## {{< fa calendar-check >}} Due Date

This assignment is due: [**{{< var md-04.homework-due >}}**]{.highlight-yellow}

## {{< fa list-check >}} Submission

No separate submission is required. Your instructor will check your GitHub repository to verify completion of the setup tasks.
